---
title: "Collaborate"
toc: false
---

# Collaborate

*I occasionally take on scoped projects, research support, tutoring, and workshops when the fit is good.*

This page exists mostly so I have somewhere to point people when they ask whether I do this for other humans too. The answer is: sometimes, yes.

I am most useful when the problem is real, the scope is clear, and the data are messy in an interesting way rather than a cursed way.

## Ways I Can Help

### Environmental data and remote sensing

Short-term help with spatial analysis, satellite imagery workflows, data wrangling, reproducible pipelines, and exploratory dashboards.

### Research support

Method advice, workflow design, code cleanup, visualization, and turning a half-finished analysis into something another person can actually follow.

### Tutoring and mentoring

Python, R, data visualization, reproducible research, remote sensing basics, and general "why is this script doing that" troubleshooting.

### Talks and workshops

Practical sessions on environmental data work, Quarto, mapping, reproducibility, or communicating technical results clearly.

## Good Fit

-   Mission-driven research, public-interest projects, and environmental work
-   Scoped collaborations, advising, tutoring, or short workshops
-   People who want honest communication, clean handoffs, and less theater

## Probably Not A Good Fit

-   Miracle timelines
-   Vague "can you AI this?" requests
-   Projects that really need a full agency or a full-time hire
-   Work that wants polish but not substance

## Logistics

I am a MEDS candidate at UCSB, so my availability is limited and changes with the quarter. If you reach out, it helps to include:

-   what you are working on
-   what kind of help you want
-   timeline
-   whether you already have data, code, or materials

## Contact

If it sounds like a fit, I can usually tell you quickly whether I have capacity.

[Email Me](mailto:ermiller@ucsb.edu?subject=Collaboration%20Inquiry)
